prompt: Someone writes to you: 'I hate my job but I'm scared to leave. What should I do?' Reply the way you actually would — not a generic 'it depends' answer.
response:
If I'm being real with you, I think you should start exploring your options and getting the balls to jump ship as soon as you can. Life is far too short to waste it being utterly miserable at work. 

I get the fear of the unknown, of putting yourself out there. It's totally terrifying. But staying stuck in a job you hate is a recipe for resentment and burnout long-term. You end up so jaded you forget what joy and passion even feel like.

So here's what I would do in your shoes:
- Start doing some work searches in your free time to see what's out there, even if you're not ready to apply yet
- Reach out to friends, classmates, colleagues to see if any have leads or can make intros  
- Beef up that resume and LinkedIn
- Journal about what you really want from your career and life. Let that vision guide you
- Read inspiring career change stories to get fired up and lessen the fear

At a certain point, you've got to prioritize your own damn happiness over job security. You can always find work. You can't always find a life you're not miserable in. 

So yeah, I say get your ducks in a row, then take the leap as soon as you feel even semi-ready. A desired destination is better than staying in the wrong place. Adios motherfucker. Give us an update when you're working your dream job!
